milestone.status: all direct submitter callsites recovered with argument-source windows
milestone.meaning: The CMOV argument selectors observed at submitter callsites are small-string inline-vs-heap storage selectors, not endpoint/auth/session success branches.

selector_pattern.bit7_predicate: MOVZX control byte; SHR AL, 7; TEST AL, AL; CMOVNZ argument, heap-slot
selector_pattern.capacity_predicate: CMP capacity slot, 0x10; LEA argument, inline-slot; CMOVNC argument, heap-slot
selector_pattern.inline_case: bit7 clear keeps the stack inline-buffer address already loaded into the argument register
selector_pattern.heap_case: bit7 set, or capacity >= 0x10, selects the heap pointer stored in the same string object slot
selector_pattern.server_implication: OpenNXT should model the submitted values as normalized strings after storage selection. These branches do not imply different protocol or auth states.

callsites.request_context: RCX object through RBX loaded from [R15]; RDX loaded from [RDX].
RCX copied from RBP; RDX copied from RSI after [RSI] load.
RCX loaded from global [0x140E58A78]; R8 is the body/payload string selected by the capacity-threshold small-string selector.
RCX copied through RBX from global [0x140E58A78].
RCX request object copied through RBX from [RDI].
RCX request object copied through RBX from [RSI].
RCX loaded from [RDI]; R8 is the RIP-relative literal address 0x140B9AC18.

next_steps: Name the non-session submitter callsite roles by correlating nearby formatter/literal windows.
Trace upstream callers of the generic JSON submit wrapper at 0x1400ED4CC because it has no embedded endpoint literal.
Use this matrix to map submitter object lifetimes and endpoint construction into OpenNXT-compatible request/session handling.
